frameworks.name: multivariate information analysis
frameworks.one_line: decomposes response into contributions from current and preceding stimuli/responses

frameworks.name: models of sequential dependencies
frameworks.one_line: framework for interpreting assimilation vs contrast effects across trials

frameworks.name: multiple regression analysis
frameworks.one_line: regression-based comparator applied to the same identification data

core_question: How do stimulus information and set size modulate sequential dependencies in absolute identification?
key_assumptions: current response is influenced by immediately preceding stimulus and response
information-theoretic decomposition can isolate sequential contributions
sensitivity measures alone (d', transmitted information) do not capture trial-to-trial coupling
manipulations: number of stimuli in the identification set
analysis method (information analysis vs multiple regression)
key_findings: sequential dependencies grew larger as stimulus information decreased
sequential dependencies grew larger as the number of stimuli increased
information-analysis and regression-analysis results were directly compared on the same data
interpretations: magnitude of sequential dependencies is governed by task difficulty factors consistent with prior literature
results constrain models of sequential dependencies in absolute identification
